goal: 150 locations across Australia, Canada, and Egypt

year_1.label: Foundation

AU.notes: Focus on capital cities and established suburban corridors with proven demand signals.

CA.notes: Enter market through Greater Toronto Area. Complete provincial franchise disclosure for Ontario and British Columbia.

EG.notes: Appoint master franchisee for Egypt. First 2 units operated by master franchisee as proof-of-concept centres.

key_milestones: Appoint Egypt master franchisee by Q1
Complete Canadian franchise disclosure documents by Q1
Open first 5 Australian centres by Q2
Launch first Canadian centre by Q3
Open first Egypt centre by Q4
Achieve 60% average class utilisation across network
Establish franchisee peer network and quarterly webinar program

year_2.label: Acceleration

AU.notes: Expand to secondary cities and outer suburban rings. Introduce multi-unit operator incentives.

CA.notes: Expand beyond initial markets. Quebec launch requires bilingual curriculum and French marketing materials.

EG.notes: Master franchisee begins sub-franchising. HQ provides curriculum and brand oversight; master handles local operations and recruitment.

key_milestones: Reach 50 cumulative centres by end of Y2
Launch multi-unit operator program
Achieve 70% average class utilisation across network
Host first annual franchisee conference
Introduce AI Fundamentals program across all centres
Establish regional manager structure (AU East, AU West, CA, EG)
Launch holiday camp program in all centres

year_3.label: Scale

AU.notes: Saturate major metros and enter regional centres with populations above 80,000. Multi-unit operators target: 30% of new openings.

CA.notes: National coverage across major English-speaking markets. Quebec operations fully bilingual.

EG.notes: Master franchisee self-sustaining. Sub-franchise network established with local training capability.

key_milestones: Reach 100 cumulative centres
Achieve 75% average class utilisation across network
Launch Teen Tech advanced program in all centres
Begin planning for Year 4-5 expansion (NZ, UAE, UK evaluation)
Multi-unit operators represent 30%+ of network
Franchise resale market established with first successful transfers
System-wide NPS score of 60+

additional_benefits: Reduced royalty rate of 7% on units 4+

benefits: Exclusive development rights for defined region
Reduced royalty rate across all units
Annual strategy retreat with executive team

master_franchisee_obligations: Maintain local training centre in Cairo
Adapt curriculum materials for local context (Arabic support materials)
Ensure regulatory compliance with Egyptian education and business law
Attend annual franchisor conference in Sydney
Submit monthly performance reports to HQ
Maintain brand standards across all sub-franchise locations
franchisor_support: Initial 10-day master franchisee training in Sydney
Curriculum and content updates delivered quarterly
Brand asset library and marketing templates
Annual on-site audit and support visit
Quarterly video strategy sessions with international expansion team

termination_triggers: Failure to meet 50% of development schedule for 2 consecutive years
Brand compliance score below 60 for 2 consecutive audits
Failure to remit royalties for 3 consecutive months

markets_under_consideration.rationale: Cultural and regulatory similarity to Australia. Strong STEM education demand. Manageable from Sydney HQ.
High disposable income, strong appetite for international education brands, English-medium market.
Large addressable market, established coding education sector, English-speaking.

evaluation_criteria: Regulatory complexity and cost of entry
Availability of qualified master franchisee candidates
HQ capacity to support additional markets

key_risks.mitigation: Maintain pipeline of 3x target candidates at all times. Offer incentives for early commitment. Reduce franchise fee for first 5 units in new markets.
Structured onboarding and 90-day launch support. Quarterly business reviews. Performance improvement plans with clear timelines. Exit assistance if unrecoverable.
Dedicated curriculum team with annual review cycle. Advisory board including educators and industry professionals. Student and parent feedback loops.

key_risks.risk: Regulatory changes in child safety or education
key_risks.mitigation: Proactive compliance monitoring in all markets. Legal counsel retained in AU, CA, and EG. Compliance buffer built into operational standards.
Royalties invoiced in local currency and converted monthly. Forward contracts considered for Egypt given EGP volatility. Platform fees collected in AUD.
Rigorous selection process. Performance milestones with exclusivity review at Year 3. Step-in rights defined in master franchise agreement. Relationship with backup candidates maintained.
